cards.title: world-model-lab
cards.direction: Episode-frame leakage in a predictive world-model is an intrinsic floor: the Bayes-risk gap between frame- and episode-level splits equals an explained-variance functional, so more frames cannot close it.
cards.note: The figure is empirical, not transported — the proof is what makes the leak a property of the partition rather than the run.

witnesses.code:
theorem bayesRisk_gap [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    (hm : m₁ ≤ m₂) (hX : MemLp X 2 μ) :
    bayesRisk m₁ X μ - bayesRisk m₂ X μ
      = μ[Var[μ[X | m₂]; μ | m₁]]

witnesses.alt: JEPA training trajectories — effective rank, KSG mutual information, validation prediction loss, and linear-probe R² across 100 epochs on dm_control Reacher, 64px vs 128px.

cards.title: Split Leakage · Effective Sample Size
cards.direction: Splitting clustered trajectory data at the frame level instead of the episode level leaves a Bayes L2 risk gap equal to an explained-variance functional of the two nested sigma-algebras; the gap does not vanish and cannot be closed by adding data within episodes. In the one-way random-effects model the variance of the grand mean decomposes exactly so that the effective sample size is the group count G, not the frame count G*m, and the Hoeffding tail of the between-group component is identical across models that differ only in frame count. A finite construction shows the method-of-moments variance-component estimate is negative with probability at least (1-2p)^G even though the true variance is 2p > 0.

witnesses.claim: The frame-vs-episode leakage gap equals an intrinsic explained-variance functional of the two sigma-algebras.
witnesses.code:
theorem bayesRisk_gap [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    (hm₁₂ : m₁ ≤ m₂) (hm₂ : m₂ ≤ m₀) (hX : MemLp X 2 μ) :
    bayesRisk m₁ X μ - bayesRisk m₂ X μ = μ[Var[μ[X | m₂]; μ | m₁]]

witnesses.claim: The episode-frame risk gap does not vanish and is not closeable by adding data within episodes.
witnesses.code:
theorem frame_split_inconsistent [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    (hX : MemLp X 2 μ) (hXm : StronglyMeasurable X) :
    bayesRisk (⊥ : MeasurableSpace Ω) X μ - bayesRisk m₀ X μ = variance X μ

witnesses.claim: Exact one-way random-effects variance decomposition: effective sample size is the group count G, not the frame count G*m.
witnesses.code:
theorem variance_grandMean_eq :
    variance M.grandMean M.μ = M.σb ^ 2 / M.G + M.σw ^ 2 / (M.G * M.m)

witnesses.claim: The Hoeffding tail of the between-group component is identical across models that differ only in frame count m.
A finite probability space where the variance-component estimate is negative with probability at least (1-2p)^G while the true variance is 2p > 0.
witnesses.code:
theorem negative_r2_prob_floor {p : ℝ} (hp0 : 0 < p) (hp1 : p < 1 / 2)
    {w : ℝ} (hw : 0 < w) :
    0 < variance effectVal (singlePMF hp0.le hp1.le).toMeasure ∧
      ENNReal.ofReal ((1 - 2 * p) ^ G)
        ≤ (jointPMF G hp0.le hp1.le).toMeasure {c | sigmaHatB w c < 0} ∧
      0 < (1 - 2 * p) ^ G

cards.title: Mutual Information · Estimator Invariance
cards.direction: True mutual information is invariant under coordinatewise measurable reparametrization, but the KSG k-nearest-neighbor estimator is not: its radius core scales linearly under uniform similarity transformations. Per-axis standardization restores invariance of the radius statistic under the full diagonal-rescaling group. These estimator-geometry failure modes, together with training non-stationarity, are witnessed on a real JEPA world model by tracking effective rank, KSG mutual-information estimates, validation prediction loss, and linear probe R^2 across training at 64px and 128px resolution on dm_control Reacher.

witnesses.claim: True mutual information is invariant under coordinatewise measurable reparametrization.
witnesses.code:
theorem mutualInformationReal_map_prodCongr
    (e₁ : α ≃ᵐ α') (e₂ : β ≃ᵐ β') (P : Measure (α × β))
    [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    (hP : P ≪ (P.map Prod.fst).prod (P.map Prod.snd)) :
    mutualInformationReal (P.map (e₁.prodCongr e₂)) = mutualInformationReal P

witnesses.claim: The KSG radius core scales linearly under uniform similarity, so the estimator is not reparametrization-invariant.
witnesses.code:
theorem ksgRadiusStat_map_smul {c : ℝ} (hcpos : 0 < c) {S : Finset (ℝ × ℝ)}
    (hS : 2 ≤ S.card) :
    ksgRadiusStat (S.map (scaleEmb hcpos.ne')) (two_le_card_map_scaleEmb hcpos.ne' hS)
      = c * ksgRadiusStat S hS

witnesses.claim: Per-axis standardization restores invariance under the full diagonal-rescaling group.
witnesses.code:
theorem standardizedRadiusStat_diagEmb_invariant {c₁ c₂ : ℝ} (h₁pos : 0 < c₁) (h₂pos : 0 < c₂)
    {S : Finset (ℝ × ℝ)} (hS : 2 ≤ S.card) (hnd : NonDegenerate S hS) :
    standardizedRadiusStat (S.map (diagEmb h₁pos.ne' h₂pos.ne')) _ _
      = standardizedRadiusStat S hS hnd

witnesses.alt: Overlay of 64px vs 128px training trajectories for effective rank, KSG mutual-information estimate, validation prediction loss, and linear probe R^2 across epochs 0-99 on dm_control Reacher
witnesses.claim: Effective rank, KSG mutual-information estimate, validation prediction loss, and linear probe R^2 across epochs 0-99 on dm_control Reacher, overlaid for 64px and 128px training runs.
